skill.name: image-outpainting
skill.description: Image outpainting on RunComfy via the `runcomfy` CLI — extend a still beyond its original canvas, fill in what the camera didn't capture, change aspect ratio (square → 16:9, portrait → landscape) while preserving the original content. Routes across Nano Banana 2 Edit (default, spatial-language driven), GPT Image 2 Edit (multi-ref with reference-style matching), FLUX Kontext Pro (single-shot maximum-preservation), and the brand edit endpoints (Seedream / Dreamina / Qwen / FLUX 2). Picks the right route based on whether the outpaint is prose-driven, reference-driven, or brand-locked. Triggers on "outpaint", "outpainting", "extend image canvas", "expand the image", "fill in around the photo", "uncrop", "change aspect ratio", "extend frame", "wide-screen from square", or any explicit ask to add canvas around an existing still.
skill.summary: Extend images beyond their original canvas using AI-powered outpainting with aspect ratio changes and identity preservation.
skill.author: doany-ai

security_audit.summary: This skill is a legitimate image outpainting wrapper for the RunComfy CLI. Static findings flagged 51 bash command examples and 23 hardcoded URLs, but all are documented CLI usage patterns. The skill uses restricted tool access (Bash(runcomfy *)), passes input via JSON flag (no shell injection), and only connects to whitelisted runcomfy.net endpoints. No malicious intent detected.

medium_findings.title: Bash Command Examples in Documentation
medium_findings.description: The skill contains multiple bash command examples (runcomfy run) in markdown documentation. These are documented usage patterns, not actual command execution. The skill restricts tool access to Bash(runcomfy *) only.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Pattern detected but these are documentation examples, not actual shell backtick syntax. The scanner misidentifies markdown code blocks as Ruby backtick execution.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs to External Documentation
low_findings.description: The skill contains hardcoded URLs to runcomfy.com documentation. These are legitimate links to the service provider and expected for a CLI wrapper skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs are documented references to the runcomfy.com service. Expected for a CLI wrapper skill.

content.value_statement: Need to expand an image beyond its original canvas or change aspect ratio? This skill uses RunComfy's AI models to extend images while preserving identity and style.
actual_capabilities: Extend image canvas in any direction (left, right, top, bottom)
Change aspect ratio (1:1 to 16:9, portrait to landscape, etc.)
Preserve original subject identity and style during extension
Use reference images to match style in extended areas
Route to appropriate model based on task type (prose-driven, reference-driven, brand-locked)
limitations: Cannot perform mask-driven local edits (use image-inpainting skill instead)
Cannot edit video canvas (use video-outpainting skill instead)
Simple prompts may produce visible seams at extension boundaries
Large extensions (>50% per pass) may degrade quality at boundaries

use_cases.title: Social media content creation
use_cases.description: Convert square product photos to 9:16 format for Reels or 16:9 for YouTube thumbnails while preserving the original subject.
use_cases.target_user: Content creators and social media managers

use_cases.title: Uncrop portrait photos
use_cases.description: Reveal more of a subject's body or environment when the original photo was cropped too tightly.

use_cases.title: Banner and hero image creation
use_cases.description: Transform a portrait shot into a wide banner or hero image by extending the canvas with matching background.

prompt_templates.title: Simple aspect ratio change
prompt_templates.prompt: Extend the canvas to a 16:9 aspect ratio by adding matching environment on the left and right sides. Continue the existing background style, lighting, and color palette. Keep the original subject exactly as in the input.
prompt_templates.scenario: When user wants to convert a square image to widescreen format

prompt_templates.title: Uncrop portrait
prompt_templates.prompt: Extend the canvas downward to show the subject's full upper body and arms. Continue the existing clothing style, lighting, and background. Keep the face and current visible area exactly as in the input.
prompt_templates.scenario: When user wants to reveal more of a cropped portrait

prompt_templates.title: Reference-style outpaint
prompt_templates.prompt: Extend the canvas on all sides. Use the style from [reference-image] to fill the new areas. Match the lighting, texture, and color palette of the original image.
prompt_templates.scenario: When user provides a reference image for style matching

prompt_templates.title: Multi-step large extension
prompt_templates.prompt: Extend the canvas by approximately 30-40% in the [direction] direction. Focus on continuing the background and environment. Keep all original content exactly as in the input.
prompt_templates.scenario: When user needs to extend an image significantly

output_examples.input: A square 1:1 photo of a person standing in front of a brick wall
output_examples.output: A 16:9 widescreen image with the same person and brick wall, extended on left and right with matching brick texture and consistent lighting

output_examples.input: A portrait photo showing only head and shoulders
output_examples.output: An extended portrait showing the full upper body, arms, and surrounding environment with consistent clothing style and background

output_examples.input: A landscape photo of a building with sky above
output_examples.output: A wider landscape with the building extended on both sides, matching sky and architectural elements

best_practices: Set the aspect_ratio parameter explicitly to lock the output canvas dimensions
Use preservation language in prompts: 'Keep the original subject exactly as in the input'
For large extensions, chain multiple passes at 30-50% per pass to avoid quality degradation
anti_patterns: Do not try to extend both sides by more than 50% in a single pass
Do not skip preservation language - Nano Banana may subtly regenerate original content
Do not use this skill for mask-driven edits inside the canvas - use image-inpainting instead

faq.question: What is image outpainting?
faq.answer: Image outpainting extends an image beyond its original canvas boundaries, filling in new areas with AI-generated content that matches the style and context of the original.

faq.question: What models does this skill use?
faq.answer: The skill routes to Nano Banana 2 Edit (default), GPT Image 2 Edit (for reference matching), or FLUX Kontext Pro (for maximum preservation) based on your task.

faq.question: Can I change any aspect ratio?
faq.answer: Yes, common ratios include 16:9, 21:9, 9:16, and 4:3. Set the aspect_ratio parameter explicitly for best results.

faq.question: Why are there visible seams in my output?
faq.answer: Simple prompts may produce seams at extension boundaries. Use reference images, FLUX Kontext Pro, or a ComfyUI outpainting workflow for better seam handling.

faq.question: Is my image data sent anywhere?
faq.answer: Images are sent only to RunComfy's model-api.runcomfy.net endpoint. No telemetry or third-party sharing occurs.

faq.question: How do I extend an image by a large amount?
faq.answer: Chain multiple outpaint passes at 30-50% per pass. Use each result as input for the next pass to avoid quality degradation.
